raw_comment: I notice that even though India has roughly four times the population compared to the United States, its electricity generation is less than half. Also, China and India have roughly the same number of citizens, yet China's electricity generation is more than four times India's. I also notice that nuclear power usage has almost doubled over the past 24 years. This makes me wonder why such large countries vary so much in their need for electricity. I also wonder if, or when, India will hop on the boat of using cleaner power. Lastly, I wonder what is seen as a "good" amount of electricity generation. Is China doing too much? Is India doing too little? Where does the U.S. fall into the mix? This doesn't necessarily relate to me and my community other than showing how much electricity my country uses. A headline that could describe this graph could be, "The Modern 'Goldilocks.' How Much is Too Much?"
ace_sentences: India has roughly four times the population compared to the United States.
The electricity generation of India is less than half of the electricity generation of the United States.
China and India have roughly the same number of citizens.
The electricity generation of China is more than four times the electricity generation of India.
The nuclear power usage has almost doubled over the past 24 years.
Large countries vary so much in their need for electricity.
I wonder why large countries vary so much in their need for electricity.
I wonder if India will hop on the boat of using cleaner power.
I wonder when India will hop on the boat of using cleaner power.
I wonder what is seen as a good amount of electricity generation.
China may be doing too much.
India may be doing too little.
I wonder where the U.S. falls into the mix.
This does not necessarily relate to me and my community.
This shows how much electricity my country uses.
A headline could describe this graph.
The headline is How Much is Too Much?
